name: Election Day Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle yeast on warm water; stir to dissolve.
2. Add 2 teaspoons sugar and 1-1/2 cups flour, beating well by hand or for 2 minutes with an electric mixer at medium speed.
3. Cover and let rise in a warm place until bubbly, about 30 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, cream butter and 1 cup sugar until light and fluffy.
5. Sift remaining 3 cups flour with salt, cinnamon, cloves, mace, and nutmeg.
6. When yeast mixture is bubbly, add eggs to the creamed butter and sugar, beating well.
7. Combine with the yeast mixture. Add the dry ingredients (flour, salt, and spices) a little at a time, beating with a spoon after each addition until smooth.
8. Stir in raisins, citron, and nuts. Pour into a well-greased and floured 10-inch tube pan.
9. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1-1/2 hours.
10. Bake in a moderate oven (375 degrees) for 1 hour. Cool in the pan for 5 minutes; turn out on a rack to finish cooling.
11. While still slightly warm, spread with confectioners' sugar frosting. Makes 12 to 16 servings.
Confectioners' Sugar Frosting:
To 1 cup sifted confectioners' sugar, add enough milk or light cream to make a spreading consistency. Add 1/2 teaspoon vanilla and a dash of salt (or flavor with 1/2 teaspoon lemon juice and 1/4 teaspoon grated lemon peel). Stir until smooth. Spread on cake.
